headline: Chips and Homebuilders Carry the Tape as Iran Strikes Rattle Energy Markets
body_html:
Wednesday's mid-session picture is broadly constructive, with advancers outpacing decliners 283 to 171 across a 456-name universe. Semiconductors lead all sectors, up 1.87%, while Homebuilders are close behind at 1.56% and Materials add another 1.05% — together providing a sturdy backbone to an otherwise uneven tape. Software and Industrials are participating modestly, leaving Energy and Healthcare as the session's laggards, with Healthcare shedding 0.54%.

The standout mover is Axcelis Technologies, surging 9.55% to pace the semiconductor-equipment space. Monolithic Power and Vicor Corporation are each adding nearly 7%, underscoring broad enthusiasm for power-delivery and chip-infrastructure names. On the building side, Louisiana-Pacific is up 6.41%, consistent with the strength in Homebuilders. The session's sharpest pain falls on Pentair, off 14.27%, while Biogen drops nearly 9% and two education-sector names — Stride and Grand Canyon Education — shed roughly 8% and 6% respectively.

Geopolitical risk is the dominant macro thread into the afternoon. U.S. strikes on Iran have escalated, threatening Hormuz shipping lanes and pushing oil prices higher, which may weigh on consumer sentiment and transport costs heading into the close. Traders will also be parsing any fresh commentary from Federal Reserve watchers ahead of the bell.

briefs.headline: Pentair Plunges to Pace Decliners on Heavy Volume
briefs.body: Pentair is Wednesday's most dramatic casualty, tumbling 14.27% mid-session in what stands as the sharpest single-stock loss across the tracked universe. The water-treatment equipment maker's slide is dragging on the broader Industrials sector, which is clinging to a slim 0.49% gain despite the headwind.

briefs.headline: U.S. Strikes on Iran Escalate, Threatening Hormuz Passage
briefs.body: Washington has launched a new wave of military strikes against Iran, and Reuters reports that Iran-linked vessels have been observed moving through the Strait of Hormuz ahead of a potential U.S. naval blockade. Oil prices are rising in response, with analysts warning that any prolonged disruption to the corridor could lift pump prices significantly.

briefs.headline: Senate Democrats Block Sweeping Defense Spending Bill Over Iran Conflict
briefs.body: Senate Democrats moved to halt a defense appropriations package worth roughly $1.15 trillion, citing objections to the widening military campaign against Iran. The procedural blockade adds legislative uncertainty to an already tense geopolitical backdrop and could complicate funding timelines for defense contractors.

briefs.headline: Buffett Cautions on Market Valuations, Backs Warsh for Fed
briefs.body: Warren Buffett, speaking publicly Wednesday, warned that identifying genuine value has grown increasingly difficult as speculative appetite dominates investor behavior. Separately, the Berkshire chairman endorsed Kevin Warsh as a sound choice for Federal Reserve chair, a comment likely to draw close attention from rates-focused market participants.

briefs.headline: Dan Ives Departs Wedbush to Launch Yorkville Ives Merchant Bank
briefs.body: Technology analyst Dan Ives, widely followed for his calls on the software and semiconductor space, has left Wedbush Securities to co-found Yorkville Ives, a new merchant banking venture. The departure removes one of Wall Street's more prominent tech bulls from the traditional sell-side research circuit.

briefs.headline: Axcelis Leads Semiconductor-Equipment Rally With Near-10% Surge
briefs.body: Axcelis Technologies is up 9.55% at midday, powering a strong session for the semiconductor-equipment sub-group. The move aligns with the broader 1.87% advance in Semiconductors — the top-performing sector Wednesday — as investors continue to favor chip-infrastructure and power-delivery names amid resilient AI-spending narratives.
